mission.title: crossplane: Granular management policies (e.g. ability to ignore changes etc.)
mission.description: Granular management policies (e.g. ability to ignore changes etc.). Requested by 39+ users.

steps.title: Check current crossplane deployment
steps.description:
Verify your crossplane version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
helm list -n crossplane 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working crossplane installation.

steps.title: Review crossplane configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant crossplane configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get configmap -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=crossplane
```
### What problem are you facing?

Crossplane provides reconciliation of all parameters under `spec`.  There are cases where this is not desired post initial creation.  Such as when a parameter is managed outside of Crossplane.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Granular management policies (e.g. ability to ignore…
steps.description:
As pointed by  [here](https://github.com/crossplane/crossplane/pull/3531#discussion_r1072945963), another solution could be something like `spec.initProvider`:

```yaml
apiVersion: eks.aws.upbound.io/v1beta1
kind: NodeGroup
metadata:
  name: sample-eks-ng
spec:
  # Desired state that will be used
```yaml
spec:
  ignoreChanges:
    - desiredCapacity
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n crossplane -l app.kubernetes.io/name=crossplane
kubectl get events -n crossplane --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Granular management policies (e.g. ability to ignore…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
As pointed by  [here](https://github.com/crossplane/crossplane/pull/3531#discussion_r1072945963), another solution could be something like `spec.initProvider`:

```yaml
apiVersion: eks.aws.upbound.io/v1beta1
kind: NodeGroup
metadata:
  name: sample-eks-ng
spec:
  # Desired state that will be used only on initial creation (optional)
  initProvider:
    scalingConfig:
      - desiredSize: 1
  #
codeSnippets:
apiVersion: eks.aws.upbound.io/v1beta1
kind: NodeGroup
metadata:
  name: sample-eks-ng
spec:
  # Desired state that will be used only on initial creation (optional)
  initProvider:
    scalingConfig:
      - desiredSize: 1
  # Desired state that will be enforced on every reconcile (optional, if the resource already exists)
  forProvider:
    scalingConfig:
      - maxSize: 3
        minSize: 1
status:
  # The observed state, updated on every reconcile.
  atProvider:
    scalingConfig:
      - desiredSize: 3
        maxSize: 3
        minSize: 1

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with crossplane installed or the issue environment reproducible.
